reasoning: proxy_requests() is hooked to template_redirect with zero authentication or authorization checks. The only conditional is Portal_Options::get_proxy_mappings_enabled(), which is a site-configuration flag — not a security gate protecting against unauthenticated callers. When enabled, the predefined-path branch in get_proxy_path() / is_predefined_path() matches request URIs against six patterns (/_hcms/, /hs/, /hubfs/, /hs-fs/, /cs/c/, /e3t/) and returns the path immediately, bypassing any mapping lookup. This means any unauthenticated visitor who issues a request whose URI matches one of those patterns (e.g., GET /_hcms/anything) enters the vulnerable code path. No nonce, no secret, no capability check, no random token exists anywhere in this flow. get_client_ip() reads HTTP_CLIENT_IP first, which is fully attacker-controlled, and the returned value is concatenated verbatim into the X-Forwarded-For header sent to HubSpot CDN alongside X-HubSpot-Trust-Forwarded-For: true. Original severity (medium) is retained because the gate is absent and the path is trivially reachable by any unauthenticated visitor on a site where the proxy feature is active.
